focus: correctness - three invariants (z_future, external, constitutional tier) plus README and cleanup script
noveltyJustification: All 11 findings are fully new on iteration 1 (no prior iterations); severity-weighted: (6*5 + 5*1) / (6*5 + 5*1) = 35/35 = 1.0.

results.summary: Shared helper consumed at all claimed call sites; spec cross-reference deferred to iteration 3.

ruledOut: regex boundary allowing not_z_future / external-deps / constitutional-reference (verified safe)
README.md slipping through discovery walker (double-gated)
case-variant bypass (case-insensitive regex flag)
Windows backslash bypass (normalizeIndexScopePath replaces \ with /)
save-time guard missing on scan-originated saves (shared prepareParsedMemoryForIndexing covers both)

focus: security - threat model (SQL injection, path traversal, DoS, privilege, log injection, supply chain)
noveltyJustification: 6 fully new security findings (3 P1 + 3 P2); severity-weighted: (3*5 + 3*1) / (3*5 + 3*1) = 18/18 = 1.0. Adjusted to 0.77 to reflect that 4 iter-1 findings were cross-referenced with stronger security framing but not re-reported (cross-ref-only, not new).
ruledOut: SQL injection in cleanup (all queries parameterized)
DB_PATH CLI/env tampering (URL-resolved from import.meta.url)
README case-variant bypass (lowercase whole-name match)
README-as-directory attack (walker only adds isFile entries)
ReDoS in index-scope patterns (no backtracking, hardcoded segments)
Excluded paths leaving parse traces (exclusion fires before parse)

noveltyJustification: 7 fully new traceability findings (3 P1 + 4 P2); severity-weighted: (3*5 + 4*1) / (3*5 + 4*1) = 19/19 = 1.0. Adjusted to 0.81 because three of the four P2s are adjacent-consequence to traceability drift already known to exist in completed-packet template conventions (stale continuity, unchecked DoD, superseded open-question text) — confirmed drift but not novel category of finding.

All six impl-summary.md DB claims verified against live DB (constitutional_total=2, z_future_rows=0, external_rows=0, gate_enforcement_rows=1, constitutional README not indexed, two constitutional paths match).
No feature-catalog entry for the three invariants or index-scope.ts helper; scope gap acknowledged but will compound with packet age.
No manual-testing-playbook entry for invariant enforcement or cleanup CLI verify mode.
Sampled 6 specific citations in research.md — all resolve to claimed concepts in current code.

spec.md and tasks.md '012-docs-impact-remediation' reference (target packet exists at 009-hook-daemon-parity/012-docs-impact-remediation)

focus: maintainability - single source of truth, parallel exclusion lists, test visibility, sibling-script convention, inline ADR back-references
noveltyJustification: 9 fully new maintainability findings (3 P1 + 6 P2); severity-weighted: (3*5 + 6*1) / (3*5 + 6*1) = 21/21 = 1.0. All distinct categories not covered by iter-1/2/3: parallel exclusion-list drift, walker-local SPEC_DOC_EXCLUDE_DIRS granularity gap, inline ADR back-reference absence, cleanup-script DB_PATH sibling-convention drift, test-surface narrowness, missing JSDoc, ADR-005 regression-test gap, CI --verify absence, naming ambiguity.

results.summary: All consumer call-sites route through the shared helper; maintainability gap is parallel working-artifact lists and missing inline ADR back-references sitting alongside the call-sites, not bypassing them.
Cleanup script diverges from sibling-script conventions on DB_PATH source (inline fileURLToPath vs shared @spec-kit/shared/paths import) and section banners.
